text: Agile methodology is a popular approach in software development. Instead of planning a whole project at once, teams work in short, repeated cycles called sprints. Each sprint typically lasts two to four weeks and ends with a working product feature. The team starts each sprint by pulling tasks from a prioritized list called the backlog. These tasks are often written as user stories, which describe a feature from the user's perspective. Every day, the team has a brief meeting known as a standup, where members share what they did yesterday, what they will do today, and any obstacles. This process is called iterative development, allowing teams to improve the product based on feedback after each sprint.
